title: kochia scoparia var. culta
intro: Kochia scoparia var. culta, also known as Mexican burning bush or burning bush, is a shrub native to Asia and introduced to North America as an ornamental plant. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various infections, including those of the skin and respiratory system. Kochia scoparia var. culta can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and its herbal preparations are often used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
